members.name: Magoo
members.focus: Sprint coordination, launch truth, sequencing, accountability.
members.kpi: Launch board current, blockers escalated, no fake completion.
members.outputSummary: Sprint board is now staffed with active work plus explicit next-up work so nobody stalls when they finish.
risks: Need daily enforcement of proof and handoff discipline

members.name: Rory
members.focus: Roadmap, scope discipline, onboarding, profile setup, product coherence.
members.kpi: Core flows fully scoped and launch-critical work clearly prioritized.
members.outputSummary: Product scope is clearer, but every core flow still needs harder acceptance criteria and finish standards.
risks: Acceptance criteria still too soft in places

members.name: Ive
members.focus: Premium visual system, hierarchy, menus, spacing, polish.
members.kpi: Visible UI consistency and premium quality across core surfaces.
members.outputSummary: Premium consistency is still behind where a public product needs to be. Major polish work remains.
risks: App still looks uneven across surfaces

members.name: June
members.focus: Navigation, onboarding, menus, profile flow, information architecture.
members.kpi: Users can move through core flows without friction or confusion.
members.outputSummary: Navigation, onboarding, and core-user flow clarity are still major launch debt.
risks: Too many decisions still feel hidden

members.name: Finch
members.focus: Next.js execution quality, page polish, responsiveness, performance.
members.kpi: Core pages stable, polished, responsive, and production-clean.
members.outputSummary: Frontend execution is solid, but the product still needs a coordinated page-by-page premium cleanup.

members.name: Slate
members.focus: Shared components, forms, tables, cards, tabs, state handling.
members.outputSummary: Shared patterns exist, but the system still allows too much one-off UI drift.

members.name: Quinn
members.focus: Regression testing, visual QA, mobile QA, blocker tracking.
members.kpi: Launch blocker count trending down with proof-based QA coverage.
members.outputSummary: QA rails exist, but launch gating is still not ruthless enough for public launch confidence.
wins: Ops board gives QA a real home

members.name: Atlas
members.focus: Data contracts, grading integrity, backend support for frontend trust.
members.kpi: Reliable data on all launch-critical surfaces.
members.outputSummary: Data rails are stronger, but trust still depends on tighter proof, verification, and consumer-facing sanity checks.
wins: Data ingestion direction is much stronger now
risks: Bad data trust would kill launch confidence

members.name: Vega
members.focus: Goose 2.0 model pipeline, systems, backtests, feature/training flow.
members.kpi: Training-ready data and honest model evaluation.
members.outputSummary: Historical ingestion rail is the strongest-moving lane right now, but it still needs hardening and evaluation discipline.
risks: Need current-state confirmation after full update

members.name: Cash
members.focus: Activation, trust surfaces, pricing, upgrade conversion.
members.kpi: Soft-launch users understand value and convert cleanly.
members.outputSummary: Conversion path is still behind product work and not ready for public pressure.

members.name: Halo
members.focus: Copy, product voice, onboarding text, premium brand tone.
members.kpi: Brand voice feels sharp, premium, and trustworthy throughout the app.
members.outputSummary: Brand direction is good, but product copy still needs a disciplined end-to-end pass.

members.name: Pulse
members.focus: Tester insight capture, confusion tracking, feedback synthesis.
members.kpi: Soft-launch feedback loop active and actionable.
members.outputSummary: Tester loop still does not exist in a usable operational form.

members.name: Forge
members.focus: Sprint cadence, launch checklist, blocker management, ship gates.
members.kpi: Clear launch board with real red/yellow/green status and zero hidden blockers.
members.outputSummary: Launch management surface exists, but cadence discipline still needs active daily pressure.
wins: Launch board now visible in admin
risks: Hidden blockers can still creep in

members.name: Ghost
members.focus: Competitor benchmarking, product pressure, UX comparison.
members.kpi: Goose closes obvious polish gap versus leading competitors.
members.outputSummary: Competitive pressure is understood, but it needs to be converted into more direct product actions.

scorecards.summary: Team board shipped into admin, CEO snapshot added, and launch management now has a real operating surface.
Historical data pipeline is now moving from theory into usable infrastructure.
Navigation and onboarding remain launch-critical UX debt.
QA admin rails exist, but the release gate needs to get stricter fast.

workstreams.title: Navigation and app shell cleanup
workstreams.goal: Make app navigation, menu logic, and shell behavior feel obvious and premium across core routes.
workstreams.proofRequired: Clean route-to-route walkthrough with no broken paths and visibly stronger hierarchy.
workstreams.notes: High urgency. June leads UX flow, Rory owns scope decisions, Ive handles hierarchy and premium shell polish.

workstreams.title: Onboarding and profile setup hardening
workstreams.goal: Make signup, first-run onboarding, and user profile setup soft-launch ready.
workstreams.proofRequired: Full happy-path walkthrough with no embarrassing friction or confusion.
workstreams.notes: Rory sets acceptance criteria, June owns UX flow, Halo cleans product copy, Finch handles implementation follow-through.

workstreams.title: Home/dashboard clarity and trust pass
workstreams.goal: Make the home experience clearly explain value, confidence, and what the user should do next.
workstreams.proofRequired: Homepage reviewed with clean hierarchy, trust language, and obvious next actions.
workstreams.notes: Halo owns narrative, Ive drives premium presentation, Rory decides product priority, Finch executes, Ghost pressure-tests versus competitors.

workstreams.title: Design system unification
workstreams.goal: Standardize cards, filters, buttons, tabs, modals, tables, and state patterns across launch-critical surfaces.
workstreams.proofRequired: Shared component set actively used across the admin and user-facing core pages.
workstreams.notes: Slate owns systemization, Ive protects visual quality, Finch ensures real adoption in the app.

workstreams.title: Picks, trends, and drill-down page polish
workstreams.goal: Bring core product pages to a soft-launch visual and functional standard.
workstreams.proofRequired: Before/after QA pass with no obvious broken states or low-rent presentation gaps.
workstreams.notes: Finch executes the pass, Ive sharpens hierarchy, Quinn validates that polish actually holds up.

workstreams.title: Loading, error, and empty-state polish
workstreams.goal: Eliminate embarrassing blank, broken, or abrupt states across key product flows.
workstreams.proofRequired: Known loading/error/empty states documented and visually cleaned up.
workstreams.notes: This gets sprinted next, but the owner/assignees are now explicit instead of hand-wavy.

workstreams.title: Data trust and grading integrity
workstreams.goal: Ensure grading outputs, pick records, and key product data are trustworthy enough for launch.
workstreams.proofRequired: No unresolved known trust issues on launch-critical data surfaces.
workstreams.notes: Atlas owns data truth, Quinn verifies, Forge makes sure unresolved blockers cannot hide.

workstreams.title: Historical data rail completion
workstreams.goal: Finish automated historical ingestion so Goose 2.0 can move into honest testing and training.
workstreams.proofRequired: Backfill current, logs clean, alerting in place, and system ready for evaluation.
workstreams.notes: Already moving. Vega closes the loop, Atlas supports data contract reliability.

workstreams.title: Launch QA gate enforcement
workstreams.goal: Establish a true launch gate with tracked blockers, mobile coverage, and proof-based QA signoff.
workstreams.proofRequired: Release gate actively used to determine go/no-go on launch-critical work.
workstreams.notes: Quinn owns QA discipline, Forge owns enforcement, Finch closes frontend failures.

workstreams.title: Soft-launch tester program
workstreams.goal: Create a controlled tester feedback loop before June 1.
workstreams.proofRequired: Named tester intake, feedback capture path, and weekly synthesis loop in place.
workstreams.notes: Pulse owns the loop, Cash turns it into conversion insight, Halo keeps tester messaging sharp.

workstreams.title: Pricing, upgrade, and conversion path
workstreams.goal: Make the upgrade path clear, credible, and non-janky before soft launch.
workstreams.proofRequired: Complete reviewed conversion flow from value discovery through upgrade CTA.
workstreams.notes: Cash owns the money path, Halo sharpens copy, Rory prevents product nonsense, Pulse closes the feedback loop.

workstreams.title: First-run decision path cleanup
workstreams.goal: Remove ambiguity from the first five minutes of use so a new user always knows what to click, why it matters, and where to go next.
workstreams.proofRequired: Fresh-user walkthrough with zero dead-end moments across home, picks, trends, and profile setup.
workstreams.notes: This is immediate follow-on work for June once nav/onboarding clears enough to move. No idle gap allowed.

workstreams.title: Visual hierarchy and spacing pass
workstreams.goal: Raise typography, spacing, density, and section hierarchy across core pages until the product feels deliberate instead of assembled.
workstreams.proofRequired: Before/after pass across homepage, picks, trends, matchup, and upgrade with visibly stronger hierarchy.
workstreams.notes: This is Ive's immediate next assignment after current shell work. Product is nowhere near Apple-level without this.

workstreams.title: Trust copy system pass
workstreams.goal: Standardize confidence language, feature framing, empty-state copy, and trust microcopy across all launch-critical surfaces.
workstreams.proofRequired: Core pages read like one coherent premium product instead of page-by-page copy drift.
workstreams.notes: Follow-on work for Halo after dashboard trust pass. This keeps copy work continuous instead of episodic.

workstreams.title: Competitor gap to action map
workstreams.goal: Turn competitor observations into a prioritized list of exact UI, trust, and product gaps to close before launch.
workstreams.proofRequired: Ranked competitor gap list tied directly to assigned product workstreams.
workstreams.notes: Ghost should not stop at observation. Competitive intel has to feed product action immediately.

workstreams.title: Mobile launch sweep
workstreams.goal: Run a brutally honest mobile-first QA sweep across launch-critical routes and document every blocker that hurts trust, clarity, or conversion.
workstreams.proofRequired: Route-by-route mobile QA board with screenshots, severity, owner, and status.
workstreams.notes: Immediate follow-on for Quinn. Public users will judge the product on mobile first.

workstreams.title: Launch blocker burn-down
workstreams.goal: Create a daily launch blocker operating loop where no blocker survives without owner, proof, and next action.
workstreams.proofRequired: Daily blocker board with aging, owner, severity, and proof of closure.
workstreams.notes: Forge gets immediate follow-on work. This is how we stop idle drift and hidden blockers.

roadmap.title: Sprint 1: Product foundation
roadmap.outcome: Navigation, dashboard clarity, and onboarding are solid enough that new users do not get lost.
roadmap.proofRequired: Core user flow walkthrough passes with premium UX and clear next actions.
roadmap.notes: This is the first impression sprint. If this looks sloppy, nothing else matters.

roadmap.title: Sprint 2: Execution system and polish rails
roadmap.outcome: Design system, page polish, and state handling stop drifting across the app.
roadmap.proofRequired: Shared patterns are visibly used across launch-critical surfaces and ugly states are cleaned up.
roadmap.notes: This is where the app stops feeling patched together.

roadmap.title: Sprint 3: Data trust and launch gate
roadmap.outcome: Data trust, QA gating, and tester feedback loop are real and usable before soft launch.
roadmap.proofRequired: Critical data surfaces verified, QA gate enforced, tester loop active.
roadmap.notes: Trust dies fast if bad data or broken flows hit testers.

sprints.name: Sprint Apr 13 to Apr 20
sprints.goal: Get product foundation and roadmap discipline in place fast enough for new hires to execute immediately.
sprints.notes: This sprint now explicitly carries the new product and design hires on concrete deliverables instead of leaving them floating.

sprints.name: Sprint Apr 20 to Apr 27
sprints.goal: Tighten execution quality, design consistency, and launch readiness across the core app.
sprints.notes: This sprint is pre-staffed with follow-on work so nobody goes idle when they finish current assignments.
